title: Gate announce-derived NETWORK location fallback behind explicit policy
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-zte2.9.2 decides that peer announce coordinates are peer-owned routing metadata and must not become this node's local NETWORK location by default. Current no-hardware gateway_network_location_submit_announce() test boundary publishes the freshest announce coordinate as NETWORK source_name=mesh-announce, but production raw announce dispatch is not wired yet. What needs to be done: add an explicit Kconfig/runtime policy gate for approximate mesh-derived location fallback before production L2 announce dispatch can feed gateway_announce_ingest_frame()/gateway_announce_ingest_verified(); default the fallback off unless a gateway profile intentionally enables it; preserve source_class=NETWORK/source_name provenance, stale withdrawal, and lower priority than fresh manual/static or local-client sources; add native_sim/qemu tests for disabled-by-default routing-only retention and enabled approximate fallback. This must be complete before project-LICHEN-9c0g wires production L2 raw announce dispatch.
notes: Review escalation from project-LICHEN-zte2.9.2: Goodall the 9th classified this as P1 because the new privacy semantics require an explicit local/build policy before peer announce coordinates can become a local NETWORK location, while current gateway code compiles announce_ingest.c/network_location.c unconditionally and gateway_announce_ingest_verified() forwards verified app_data to gateway_network_location_submit_announce(), which publishes source_name=mesh-announce. This Bead is a production blocker and blocks project-LICHEN-9c0g. Do not wire production L2 raw announce dispatch until this policy gate and disabled-by-default tests exist.
